epic.provisional_name: named per bdo's carry-over brief (decision A/B): Scout → Claim → Build → Prune; the rename of the earlier working epic.explore-lobe
epic.arc: The loop grows its missing half and the organs that keep it lawful. Every organ ontum has is an uncertainty-terminator — a gate cuts, a fold collapses, term_economy classifies, the grip discipline refuses the unproven, the owner's stamp is the final cut. That is a fully-built convergent (exploit) lobe and no divergent (explore) one. This epic builds a small strategy metabolism — Scout → Claim → Build → Prune — where Scout reads priors toward a handed purpose+goal and emits one marked, expiring, reversible call to action that moves the system forward without closing the future; Claim bounds that conjecture into work; Build is the existing exploit loop; Prune retires dead possibility. The payout is not capital (the future banked, the most aggressive terminator, one point of one axis) — it is motion: the draw that never spends the principal.
epic.context: Born in the design conversation of 2026-06-19 (captured in explore-lobe.proposal.md) and reframed by bdo's carry-over brief of 2026-06-20 (the doctrine note strategy-metabolism.md): the explore lobe must not land alone — exploration creates an immediate need for claiming and pruning, or the system grows beautiful overgrowth. The forward-looking twin of loop/gaps.py: where gaps emits calls to action from the certain present (the maintenance backlog), Scout emits them from research over priors toward a goal (the opportunity frontier). Read-only and propose-only throughout (D-4): Scout never claims, only holds; every output is marked conjecture (status:conjecture, review_required, truth_claim:false, minted:false) and can never land, mint, or alter truth — it develops and requisitions review, the merge-node and bdo's confirm-arc land. Three forks stay bdo's (strategy-metabolism.md §11): A/B the names (adopted: Scout, Scout→Claim→Build→Prune), C persistence (adopted: marked expiring conjecture), D self-aiming (adopted: handed-not-self-aiming in v1).
epic.value: The system stops only ever reducing. It gains a lawful place for pre-evidence motion — it can say, from held-open uncertainty, 'here is one move worth making toward this purpose that is not on the record yet', priced, reversible, and expiring — without polluting truth, because the move is marked conjecture. And it gains the organs (Claim, Prune) that keep that motion from becoming unmanaged growth.
epic.horizon: A read-only Scout that, given a handed purpose+goal, folds priors and emits one marked ScoutCTA (schema-shaped, grounded, expiring); a Claim organ that bounds a CTA into an arc/spike/watch; the existing Build loop; and a Prune organ that retires expired or duplicated possibility. The convergent loop already produces and capitalizes; this is the explore front that feeds it and the pruning that keeps it lawful.

pieces.glue: the Scout lobe's read-only fold: pointed at a handed purpose+goal, it researches priors (the log, the deterministic folds, the causality relation graph) and emits one marked call to action with cited real antecedents, refusing any whose antecedents do not resolve (the ghost refusal, reused from causality — no second truth). loop/explore.py is its v0 read-only fold (done-line 0140); loop/scout_cta.py is the ScoutCTA contract it emits under (done-line 0141, the §6 schema: conjectural, expiring, reversible, priced, non-minted). The generating loop/scout.py that joins the fold to the schema is a later line.

the Claim organ (strategy-metabolism.md §7): converts a conjectural ScoutCTA into bounded work (arc / epic / spike / branch / watch / defer). It does not prove the CTA — it says 'this frontier is bounded enough to work or watch'. The missing bridge between possibility and execution; a later line.

the Prune organ (strategy-metabolism.md §8): retires open possibility consuming field-space — an expired conjecture, a noise signal, a duplicated branch, a frontier that is real but not ours. Not a gate (a gate judges what tries to land; Prune judges open possibility). Without it, Scout creates unmanaged growth; a later line.
